_meta.what_firm_does: LangChain builds the foundation for agent engineering—helping developers go from prototypes to production AI agents. $125M Series B (IVP, Sequoia, Benchmark, CapitalG, Sapphire). Platform: LangSmith, open-source LangChain/LangGraph/Deep Agents. 100M+ monthly OSS downloads; 5 of Fortune 10 use LangSmith. SF + NY offices.
_meta.jd_wants: On-the-ground People Ops + Workplace execution in SF: front of house/office env; onboarding (Rippling HRIS setup, access, equipment, Day-1) + offboarding; benefits admin; immigration logistics (H-1B/OPT/PERM); payroll inputs; HRIS hygiene; events/culture; SOPs/Notion wiki; identify workflows to automate with AI; use LangChain's own tooling. 2-4 yrs office mgmt/People Ops/HR; Rippling preferred; high ownership; warmth.
_meta.drafted_by: Opus 4.8 in Jamie's voice, mined from voice corpus + feedback rules
_meta.status: DRAFT — Jamie to review/edit before submit

Why are you interested in this role?: This role is a near-perfect match for how I actually work. I'm a high-ownership person who notices what's broken and just fixes it—and I love the breadth here: onboarding logistics, HRIS hygiene, events, and building the systems that let a People function scale. At InGenius Prep I run onboarding and Day-1 readiness (down 75% in onboarding time), maintain our SOPs and Notion wiki, and actively look for repetitive workflows to automate with AI. LangChain being a build environment, not a maintain one, is exactly where I do my best work, and getting to use your own tooling to make my job smarter is a genuine bonus.
Tell us about your People Ops / HRIS exp: Across my roles I've done the operational core of People Ops: at InGenius I own onboarding logistics, system setup, and Day-1 readiness for 70+ staff and keep documentation clean and audit-ready; at Vestas I optimized onboarding across 3 global locations and supported HR operations including benefits-related coordination. I've used Rippling along with SAP and ADP, and I'm relentless about HRIS hygiene—my supervisor has called me the most organized person he's ever worked with. I haven't owned every piece (e.g., running payroll independently), but I pick up systems fast and treat clean data as the foundation of a team's trust.
How do you think about using AI in your: I'm genuinely the person on a team who reaches for AI first. I use AI tools daily, and I've been building a RAG-based system on the side that evaluates job fit from my own resumes and experience—so I think about AI concretely. In a People Ops role, I'd look for the repetitive, manual workflows (onboarding checklists, document tracking, drafting templated comms, surfacing HRIS data issues) and propose AI-assisted ways to eliminate them, exactly the way this role describes. I'd also be excited to learn LangChain's own tooling and use it on the job.
How do you feel about supporting immigra: I'd handle it with real care—partly because I'm navigating the H-1B process myself, so I understand firsthand how much accurate document collection and deadline tracking matters to the person on the other end. Coordinating with external counsel, tracking deadlines, and keeping records clean is exactly the kind of detail-oriented, high-trust work I'm good at, and I know how stressful these processes can feel, so I'd bring patience and warmth to it.
Why LangChain?: I'm drawn to LangChain because you're at the center of how AI agents show up in the real world, and because this role has genuine breadth and ownership rather than being narrow back-office work. I love that you want someone who builds rather than waits to be assigned—that's me. My training in Applied Organizational Psychology means I treat People operations as a system to study and improve, and I'd be excited to be the person who makes both your office and your People function run beautifully as you scale.
